prompt:
SRE ticket. Our DR tooling snapshots Terraform Enterprise state by scraping the UI with a headless browser (yes, really), and it broke again when the login page changed. Replace it with a small TypeScript tool that pulls a workspace's current state through the documented v2 API. Node built-ins only — no npm installs, no package.json; the test runner does type stripping, so keep the TS erasable.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Terraform Enterprise API documentation on developer.hashicorp.com — the API overview page (auth, JSON:API conventions, error documents, 404 semantics) and the /state-versions reference. One thing to check carefully while you're in there: an old internal note claims the state-version show response includes an md5 checksum you can verify — when I read the current page, md5 only exists as a create-request attribute and the show response has neither md5 nor lineage, which is why our integrity plan is the size attribute plus a serial cross-check. Confirm that against the live docs and say what you find. Our pinned contract is docs/contract.json (provenance in docs/official_sources.json); both protected. If live official docs contradict them, report the conflict in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Shape of the tool (the tests import from ./client.ts and ./download.ts; add more modules if you want, those two are the public face):

client.ts
- class TfeClient(baseUrl, token) — every /api/v2 request carries the bearer header. resolveWorkspace(org, name) hits the documented organization/name workspace show and returns {id, currentStateVersionId} from the current-state-version relationship (null when the workspace has never stored state). getStateVersion(id) returns {id, status, serial, size, resourcesProcessed, downloadUrl, jsonDownloadUrl} from the documented attributes (size/jsonDownloadUrl null when absent).
- class ApiError for any non-2xx JSON:API response: statusCode plus the parsed errors array, message carrying the detail. TFE 404s mean 'missing OR unauthorized' — keep that wording intact.

download.ts
- downloadHostedState(client, url) — GET the hosted-state-download-url. It is pre-signed; on some installs it sits on the API host, on others on a separate archivist host, and it may redirect cross-host. Policy (pinned in docs/contract.json): attach the bearer ONLY when the request origin is exactly the TFE API origin; follow redirects manually, at most 3 hops, re-deciding the auth question per hop — the token must never travel to a foreign origin. More than 3 redirects is an error.
- downloadCurrentState(client, org, name, destPath) — resolve the workspace (NoStateError when there's no state), fetch the state version, refuse anything not finalized (StateNotReadyError, without issuing a download request), download, verify integrity, and write atomically: temp file in the destination directory, rename into place, never leave a temp file behind. Integrity: when the size attribute is present the byte count must match exactly; the payload parses as JSON and its serial must equal the state version's serial attribute. Any mismatch is an IntegrityError and nothing gets written — an existing destination stays byte-identical. Returns {stateVersionId, serial, bytesWritten, path}.

Acceptance is test_state_download.ts — loopback fake TFE and fake archivist servers, no real credentials. Run: node --test test_state_download.ts — until green. test_state_download.ts and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: Terraform Enterprise REST API v2 (/api/v2, JSON:API): workspaces and state-versions endpoints (stable)

official_sources.title: API documentation for Terraform Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: /api/v2 base path, Authorization: Bearer token auth, application/vnd.api+json JSON:API conventions, JSON:API errors array shape, and 404 responses masking unauthorized resources

official_sources.title: /state-versions API reference for Terraform Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/current-state-version and GET /state-versions/:state_version_id; hosted-state-download-url / hosted-json-state-download-url as temporary pre-signed archivist URLs; serial, size, status (pending/finalized/discarded), resources-processed; and the absence of md5/lineage in the show attributes (md5 is a create-request attribute only)

research.language_rationale: There is no official HashiCorp TypeScript SDK for the TFE API; the documented integration surface is the raw JSON:API REST contract at /api/v2, and Node's built-in fetch/http plus node:fs is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to implement a downloader that needs manual redirect control and atomic writes.
